ClassGearSets.brief: A collection of constants representing distinct gear set configurations for various character classes.
ClassGearSets.details: The ClassGearSets group defines a set of constants that serve as unique identifiers for different gear configurations associated with specific character classes in the game. These gear sets include Mage Invoker, Warrior Berserker, Cleric Divine, Thief Cutpurse, and Paladin Grace, each tailored to enhance or modify gameplay based on class-specific attributes and abilities. The constants facilitate organized and consistent referencing of gear sets across various systems, enabling efficient application of set bonuses, effects, and class-specific behaviors during gameplay. They are integral to the game's equipment management, character customization, and effect application mechanisms.
ClassGearSets.notes: The constants are organized sequentially with unique integer values, ensuring easy identification and maintenance. They are used as standardized identifiers throughout the codebase to promote consistency in gear set recognition and effect application.
ClassGearSets.rationale: This group exists to centralize the identification of class-specific gear configurations, simplifying the management and application of gear-related effects. By providing clear and maintainable constants, it supports modularity and reduces errors in referencing gear sets, thereby enhancing the system's robustness and scalability.
ClassGearSets.name: ClassGearSets

SET_MAGE_INVOKER.brief: The constant `SET_MAGE_INVOKER` identifies the gear set configuration for mage characters specializing in invocation magic.
SET_MAGE_INVOKER.details: This constant holds the integer value 43 and is used throughout the codebase to represent the specific gear set known as 'Mage Invoker'. It is referenced in switch statements and conditional logic to determine whether a character is equipped with this gear set, which affects various gameplay mechanics such as attribute bonuses, spellcasting speed, and damage modifications. The constant ensures consistent identification and handling of the Mage Invoker set across different systems, including effect descriptions, attribute checks, and damage calculations. Its usage is integral to applying the correct effects and behaviors associated with this particular gear configuration.
SET_MAGE_INVOKER.notes: The constant is typically used as a case label in switch statements and in conditional checks to verify if a character has the Mage Invoker set equipped. It is stored as a constexpr integer for compile-time efficiency and consistency.
SET_MAGE_INVOKER.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify the Mage Invoker gear set within the game's logic, enabling consistent application of set-specific effects and behaviors across various gameplay systems.
SET_MAGE_INVOKER.name: SET_MAGE_INVOKER

SET_WARRIOR_BESERKER.brief: Constant that uniquely identifies the 'Warrior Berserker' gear set within the game.
SET_WARRIOR_BESERKER.details: The `SET_WARRIOR_BESERKER` constant is an integer value (44) used as a unique identifier for the 'Berserker' gear set assigned to warrior characters. It is referenced throughout the codebase to determine if a character has equipped this specific set, which in turn activates various special effects such as stamina cost reductions, descriptive effect labels, and combat bonuses related to the berserker attribute. This constant ensures consistent and efficient recognition of the gear set across different modules, including attribute checks, effect descriptions, and combat mechanics, facilitating maintainability and clarity in the game's logic.
SET_WARRIOR_BESERKER.notes: The constant is primarily used as a case label in switch statements and as a check condition in functions that modify character behavior based on gear set presence. Its fixed value allows for straightforward comparisons and reduces the risk of errors in gear set identification.
SET_WARRIOR_BESERKER.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, centralized identifier for the 'Berserker' gear set, enabling game mechanics to easily recognize and apply effects associated with this specific equipment configuration, thereby supporting consistent gameplay behavior and effect management.
SET_WARRIOR_BESERKER.name: SET_WARRIOR_BESERKER

SET_CLERIC_DIVINE.brief: Identifier for the Cleric Divine gear set used to recognize divine attribute bonuses.
SET_CLERIC_DIVINE.details: The constant `SET_CLERIC_DIVINE` holds the integer value 45, serving as a unique identifier for the divine gear set associated with cleric characters. It is utilized throughout the game's codebase, particularly in spell and healing functions, to verify whether a character has equipped or benefits from divine gear or attributes. This check influences healing calculations and other divine-related effects, ensuring that bonuses are applied correctly when a character's gear or attributes match this set. Its consistent use helps maintain the integrity of divine attribute checks across various gameplay mechanics.
SET_CLERIC_DIVINE.notes: The constant is primarily used as a key in attribute or gear set verification processes, facilitating quick identification of divine bonuses in character data.
SET_CLERIC_DIVINE.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to reference the divine gear set associated with clerics, enabling consistent application of divine bonuses and effects during gameplay, especially in healing and buffing spells.
SET_CLERIC_DIVINE.name: SET_CLERIC_DIVINE

SET_THIEF_CUTPURSE.brief: Identifier for the 'Thief Cutpurse' gear set within the game's gear set system.
SET_THIEF_CUTPURSE.details: The constant `SET_THIEF_CUTPURSE` holds a specific integer value (46) that uniquely identifies the 'Thief Cutpurse' gear set in the game's data structures. It is used throughout the codebase to reference this particular set when applying effects, checking attributes, or generating descriptive information related to the Thief class's specialized equipment. Its consistent use ensures that all modules, such as combat mechanics, location descriptions, and attribute checks, correctly recognize and handle the 'Cutpurse' gear set, facilitating modular and maintainable code.
SET_THIEF_CUTPURSE.notes: This constant is part of a larger enumeration of gear set identifiers, enabling efficient switch-case handling and attribute verification related to gear sets.
SET_THIEF_CUTPURSE.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify the 'Thief Cutpurse' gear set across various systems, supporting consistent application of set-specific effects and bonuses during gameplay.
SET_THIEF_CUTPURSE.name: SET_THIEF_CUTPURSE

SET_PALADIN_GRACE.brief: Constant identifier for the 'Paladin Grace' attribute set within the game's gear configuration.
SET_PALADIN_GRACE.details: The `SET_PALADIN_GRACE` constant, defined as 47, uniquely identifies the 'Paladin Grace' attribute set used in the game's character gear system. It is referenced throughout the codebase to verify whether a character has this specific attribute set active, which influences various gameplay mechanics such as maximum hit points and healing effects during combat actions like blur, parry, and shield block. By serving as a consistent identifier, this constant facilitates the application of grace-related bonuses and effects, ensuring that character attributes are correctly evaluated and applied during gameplay interactions.
SET_PALADIN_GRACE.notes: This constant is primarily used in attribute checks to trigger grace-related effects. Its fixed value ensures reliable identification across different functions and modules that handle character attribute validation.
SET_PALADIN_GRACE.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to reference the 'Paladin Grace' attribute set, enabling consistent application of associated effects and simplifying attribute verification logic within the game's combat and gear systems.
SET_PALADIN_GRACE.name: SET_PALADIN_GRACE
